response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong web, full-stack, and some systems-level background (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux). However, they lack nearly all core requirements for this role: no native-level Japanese (languages listed are English, Romanian, German), no embedded networking or LAN-switch experience, no Linux kernel investigation/modification work, and no demonstrated security-technology background (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption). Their C experience is dated and desktop-tooling oriented rather than UNIX/embedded firmware development.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware contexts
Prior C/C++ development experience on a professional IDE product (NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement for this role
No experience with network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 switch development
No Linux kernel analysis/modification experience; recent work is web/TypeScript, far from embedded firmware in C

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering seniority and some C/C++ background
